stories.title: Task Reference URL
stories.description:
Add a single reference URL field to each task, displayed directly below the title in the task detail panel.

**Database:**
- Add `reference_url TEXT` column to `tasks` table via migration

**Detail Panel UI:**
- Below the title (data-detail-field="0"), add a reference URL field (data-detail-field between title and status)
- Empty state: subtle "Add reference..." placeholder text, same style as other muted placeholders
- With URL: display as clickable link (truncated to domain + path), with an X button to clear
- Click the link → opens in default browser via shell.openExternal
- URLs without http:// or https:// prefix are auto-prefixed with https:// when opened (so www.google.com works)
- Auto-save with 1s debounce (same pattern as title and description)
- In the Tab order between title and status

**Edge cases:**
- Very long URLs: truncated display with ellipsis, full URL shown on hover (title attribute or CSS tooltip)
- Invalid/non-URL text: stored as-is but not clickable (no underline, no click handler)
- Empty string after clearing: stored as null

**Keyboard:**
- Tab from title → reference URL field → status
- Enter in the field: blur (same as title)
- Escape: blur without saving

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Task detail panel shows a reference URL field below the title
Typing a URL saves it to the task with 1s debounce autosave
Clicking a saved URL opens it in the default browser
URLs without http/https prefix are auto-prefixed with https:// when opened
X button clears the URL
Field is keyboard-accessible and in the Tab order between title and status
URL persists across app restarts (stored in SQLite via reference_url column)
Works in both project views and My Day

stories.title: Calendar View
stories.description:
A new cross-project Calendar view that shows all tasks organized by their due date. Accessible from the sidebar between My Day and Projects (⌘2). Supports both monthly grid and weekly layout with a toggle.

**Sidebar & Navigation:**
- Add 'Calendar' to sidebar after My Day with Calendar icon from lucide-react
- Keyboard shortcut ⌘2 — bump Projects to ⌘3+, Archive to ⌘4, Templates to ⌘5
- Add 'calendar' to ViewId type and VIEW_ORDER in viewStore.ts
- Add conditional render in AppLayout.tsx

**Monthly View:**
- Classic 7-column grid (Mon–Sun), each cell lists tasks due that day
- Navigate months with left/right arrow buttons in the header
- Today highlighted with accent color/border
- Each task shows: colored project dot + title (truncated if long)
- Done tasks show strikethrough + muted text
- Overdue tasks (past due, not done) show in red

**Weekly View:**
- 7-day horizontal columns with more vertical space per day
- Scrollable if many tasks
- Navigate weeks with left/right arrow buttons
- Same task display as monthly

**Toggle:**
- Month/Week toggle button in the header area
- Persist the user's choice via settings store (key: 'calendar_layout')

**Interactions:**
- Click task → select it and open detail panel (same as project views)
- Drag task between day cells → updates due_date to the target day
- Right-click task → context menu (same as other views)

**Scope:**
- Cross-project: shows ALL non-archived, non-template tasks that have a due_date
- Project color dot on each task for identification
- Tasks without due_date do NOT appear

**Empty States:**
- Days with no tasks: empty cell
- No tasks with due dates at all: centered empty state message

**Files to create/modify:**
- Create `src/renderer/src/features/views/CalendarView.tsx`
- Modify `src/renderer/src/shared/stores/viewStore.ts` — add 'calendar' to ViewId and VIEW_ORDER
- Modify `src/renderer/src/AppLayout.tsx` — add conditional render, view title, view counts
- Modify `src/renderer/src/features/sidebar/Sidebar.tsx` — add Calendar nav item
- Modify keyboard shortcut mappings for ⌘2-5

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Calendar view accessible from sidebar with Calendar icon (⌘2)
Monthly grid shows tasks on their due date with project color indicators
Weekly view shows tasks in day columns with more vertical space
Toggle between month and week views persists across sessions
Click a task opens the detail panel
Drag a task between days updates its due_date
Left/right arrows navigate between months (monthly) or weeks (weekly)
Today is visually highlighted with accent color
Overdue tasks show in red
Tasks without due dates don't appear in the calendar
Empty state when no tasks have due dates
Works with existing detail panel, context menu, and keyboard shortcuts

stories.title: Template Wizard — Relative Due Date Offsets
stories.description:
When saving a project as a template, the wizard asks whether to include due dates as relative offsets. When deploying, compute actual due dates from a deploy date.

**Save flow:**
- Add a 'duedates' wizard step (between tasks and review) — only shown if any tasks have a due_date
- Step shows: "Transfer due dates as relative offsets?" toggle (default on)
- Offset = Math.round((taskDueDate - today) / msPerDay)
- If toggled off, all due_date_offset values are set to null

**Deploy flow:**
- Add a 'duedates' wizard step — only shown if any tasks have due_date_offset !== null
- Step shows: deploy date picker (defaults to today) + preview list with computed dates
- When creating tasks, set due_date = deployDate + due_date_offset days (ISO 8601)

**Files to change:**
- src/shared/types.ts — add due_date_offset: number | null to ProjectTemplateTask type
- src/renderer/src/AppLayout.tsx — in buildTaskTree, compute due_date_offset from task.due_date
- src/renderer/src/features/templates/DeployProjectTemplateWizard.tsx — add duedates step, StepDueDates component, apply offset in createTaskTree

**Edge cases:**
- Tasks with no due date → due_date_offset: null, skip in deploy
- Negative offsets are valid (overdue relative to save date)
- Subtasks carry offsets too
- Existing templates without due_date_offset → treated as null (backward compat)

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Save wizard shows due dates step when tasks have due dates
Toggle controls whether offsets are included in the template
Deploy wizard shows due dates step when template has offsets
Deploy date picker defaults to today
Preview shows computed dates for each task
Created tasks get correct due_date based on deploy date + offset
Subtasks carry offsets too
Existing templates without offsets work normally (backward compat)
Tasks without due dates are skipped

stories.title: Due Date Notifications
stories.description:
Native macOS notifications that fire when a task's due date+time approaches. Only tasks with a specific time (not date-only) trigger notifications.

**Notification timing:**
- Configurable lead time: 5, 15, 30, or 60 minutes before (default: 15 min)
- Always fires at 1 minute before due (when notifications enabled)
- Only tasks with due_date containing 'T' (time component) trigger notifications

**Settings:**
- New 'Notifications' section in Settings (General tab)
- Enable/disable toggle (default: enabled)
- Lead time dropdown: 5 min, 15 min, 30 min, 1 hour

**Implementation:**
- Uses Electron's Notification API for native macOS notifications
- Title: task title, Body: 'Due in X minutes' or 'Due in 1 minute'
- Main process checks every 60 seconds for upcoming due tasks
- Track which notifications have been sent to avoid duplicates (Set of taskId+time combos)
- Click notification → bring app to front, navigate to task's project, select task, open detail panel

**Edge cases:**
- Task completed before notification → skip
- Task due_date changed → re-evaluate on next check cycle
- Multiple tasks due at same time → one notification per task
- Date-only tasks (no time component) → no notification
- App not running → no notifications

Before marking passes: true: read ui-reference.md and debug-learnings.md. Write tests for any new repository methods or utility functions. Run npm run test — all existing and new tests must pass. Run npm run typecheck — zero errors.
acceptance_criteria: Native macOS notification fires at configurable lead time before due
Second notification fires at 1 minute before due
Only tasks with due_date+time (contains T) trigger notifications
Clicking notification focuses app, navigates to task project, opens detail panel
Settings toggle enables/disables notifications
Settings dropdown configures lead time (5/15/30/60 min)
Completed/archived/template tasks don't trigger notifications
No duplicate notifications for the same task+time
